extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (priority 50), whose guard is the conjunction of all 8 conditions including polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true by r3 (priority 50) whose guard is simply polypharmacy. These rules target different outputs (a vs b), so priority/tie-breaking conflict resolution never suppresses either one. When all 8 of r2's conjuncts are true, r2 fires (rec_drug_a=true) and, because polypharmacy is among them, r3 also fires (rec_drug_b=true). No rule or default sets either output false once a matching rule fires. Thus the counterexample with polypharmacy, bradycardia, renal_impairment, qt_prolongation, immunosuppressed, hyperkalemia, hepatic_impairment, peptic_ulcer all true makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. (elderly only affects rec_drug_c via r1 and is irrelevant; set to false.)
